fixture.description: A new CSV exporter writes a user-supplied free-text `label` into each row. The escapeField helper correctly RFC-4180-quotes fields (doubles quotes, wraps values containing comma/quote/newline) but emits everything else verbatim. A label that starts with =, +, -, or @ contains no quoting trigger, so it passes through unquoted and becomes a live formula when the file is opened in Excel/Sheets (CSV/formula injection, CWE-1236). The RFC-4180 quoting itself is correct and is NOT the bug. The bug is introduced by the change: the `before` file has only the (correct) escapeField/toCsvRow helpers; the `after` adds the exportOrders function that funnels the attacker-influenced label through escapeField alone, so the formula-injection sink lands squarely in the new lines while the quoting logic stays visible as a precision decoy.

grader.rubric: PASS only if the report's MAIN section (not an appendix) flags that exportOrders writes the user-supplied `label` into the CSV without neutralizing spreadsheet formula triggers, so a label beginning with =, +, -, or @ (escapeField does NOT quote it because it contains no comma/quote/newline) is emitted verbatim and becomes an executable formula when the file is opened in Excel/Google Sheets — i.e. CSV / formula / spreadsheet injection (CWE-1236). It must name the fix: prefix a single quote (or otherwise neutralize) when a string field starts with one of = + - @ (tab/CR acceptable too). Severity must be IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if this is missing, only in an appendix, below IMPORTANT, dropped as pre-existing, or if the report instead claims the RFC-4180 quoting (the double-quote escaping / comma-and-newline wrapping in escapeField) is the defect — that quoting is correct.

expected_behavior: Bugs & Security agent flags the user-controlled `label` reaching the CSV as a formula-injection sink (CWE-1236)
Notes that escapeField only RFC-4180-quotes special chars, so =/+/-/@ values pass through verbatim and execute as formulas in a spreadsheet
Recommends prefixing a single quote (or equivalent neutralization) for fields starting with = + - @ (tab/CR)
Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL, in the main report
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim the RFC-4180 quote-doubling / comma-and-newline wrapping in escapeField is the bug (it is correct)
Does NOT require a proof-of-concept spreadsheet to flag it
Does NOT dismiss it because the value is only dangerous once opened in a spreadsheet client
